scenarios.scenario_name: Athlete with overreaching markers — needs deload

constraint_adherence.reasoning: 6 days/week with Thu rest matches days_per_week=6. Week 1 has 2 lift days (Tue, Fri) and Week 2 has 2 lift days (Tue, Sun), matching lifting_days=2. Cardio types include run and bike as specified. Recent overreaching markers explicitly referenced in narrative and conditional gates.

periodization.reasoning: Week 1 cuts volume ~38% (stated and reflected: 35+40+14+30 = ~119min run vs recent 5x avg). Quality work fully suspended in Week 1. Week 2 reload is conditional on HRV ≥52 and sleep trend, with 0% volume increase — appropriately conservative. Phase labeled base with deload framing fits the unplanned recovery context.

intensity_distribution.reasoning: Week 1 is 100% Z1-Z2 across all run/bike sessions (Mon 35min Z2, Wed bike Z1-2, Sat 14km Z2, Sun 30min Z2) — well above 85% easy threshold. Week 2 has exactly one quality session (Fri threshold 3x5min) placed after Thu rest with 48h+ clearance from Tue's lift, and Sat long run is easy Z2. Hard/easy alternation respected.

specificity.reasoning: Every session specifies duration/distance, pace (e.g., Week 1 Mon 30min @ 5:50/km Z2; Week 2 Fri 3x5min @ 4:15/km Z4), HR zone, and rationale. Lifts list exercise×sets×reps×RPE (e.g., Week 1 Tue Flat DB Bench 3x10 RPE6). Conditional logic for Week 2 quality session is explicit.

scenario_fit.reasoning: All must_haves met: Week 1 volume cut ~38% (in 30-40% range), zero quality sessions in Week 1, ~100% easy volume, Week 2 reload is explicitly conditional on recovery markers, narrative and risks section reference HRV/sleep/RPE markers extensively. No must_not_have violations: Week 1 long run is 14km (≤15km), no heavy lifting (RPE 6-7 cap on upper-only work), single quality session is in Week 2 not Week 1.

strengths: Explicit conditional gating for Week 2 reload tied to specific HRV (≥52) and sleep (≥6.5h) thresholds — proper autoregulation
Week 1 Sat long run at exactly 14km / 5:55/km respects the ≤15km cap and keeps effort genuinely easy; Week 1 lifts confined to upper-body at RPE 6-7
Risks section directly addresses the athlete's high easy-run HR (162 bpm at 4.9/km) and prescribes a corrective slower pace (5:40-5:55/km)
weaknesses: Week 2 Tue full-body lift includes RDL at RPE7 and the same day a bike spin follows Wed — fine, but Fri threshold is only 60h after Tue RDL; with active overreaching markers, a slightly lighter lower-body reintro (skip RDL or keep at RPE6) would be safer
Week 2 holds volume flat which is conservative and appropriate, but the plan could specify a fallback Week 2 volume cut if recovery hasn't normalized rather than just 'mirror Week 1'
Week 1 Sun easy run (30min) the day after a 14km long run is acceptable but adds back-to-back run days during a deload; a second bike or full rest might recover faster

scenarios.plan_summary:
Split: upper_lower
Narrative: **Unplanned Recovery Block — Two Red Flags Active**

This 2-week plan is a mandatory, unplanned deload in response to two simultaneous autoregulation red flags: HRV has cratered 28% below the 7-day rolling average (42 vs. 58) and sleep is averaging 6.1h against a normal 7.5h baseline. Leg heaviness and elevated RPE on easy runs confirm accumulated fatigue — likely the tail end of a loading block that ran too long.

**Week 1 — Full Deload:** Volume is cut ~38% from recent norms. All Zone 4-5 (threshold, VO2max) work is suspended. No quality run sessions. Running is capped at easy Zone 2 efforts (target ~5:40–5:55/km, ~10-15% slower than recent 4.9 avg pace). Long run is kept short (~14 km at easy pace — bottom end of the 1.5x avg distance range). Lifting drops to 2 sessions, upper-body accessory focus at RPE 6-7 max; no heavy barbell squats or deadlifts until HRV rebounds. Total weekly running volume is held well below recent levels. Bike is used as active recovery (Zone 1-2 only).

**Week 2 — Cautious Return:** If HRV has returned to within 10% of the 7-day baseline (≥52) AND sleep has trended above 6.5h for at least 3 consecutive nights by the end of Week 1, Week 2 reintroduces modest volume (+0% cap strictly honored) and one short quality run (a single controlled threshold session). If recovery markers have NOT normalized, Week 2 mirrors Week 1. One lower-body lift is reintroduced in Week 2 at conservative loads (RPE 7 max). The athlete should self-regulate: if any two red flags co-occur mid-week, convert that day to full rest.

**Pacing Reference:** Easy/Z2 run target: 5:40–5:55/km. Long run: 5:50–6:00/km. Threshold (Week 2 only, if cleared): ~4:10–4:20/km. Bike: Zone 2 = 55-65% FTP.

**Priority above all else: sleep.** If sleep remains below 6.5h through another 3+ consecutive nights, add a full rest day and do not attempt any quality work regardless of which week of the plan we are in.
Phase: base
Deload every: 4 weeks
Risks called out by planner: HRV + sleep double red flag is still active at plan start — if both remain unresolved by Day 4, Week 1 running volume should be cut a further 15% and the single quality session in Week 2 should be abandoned entirely. | Athlete's recent easy-run HR of 162 bpm at 4.9 min/km is high for an 'easy' effort — true Zone 2 pace is likely 5:40-5:55/km; running faster than this during deload will extend, not resolve, the overreaching state. | Reintroducing lower-body strength in Week 2 carries residual injury/fatigue risk — if leg heaviness persists beyond Day 10, keep lifting upper-body only and delay squats/deadlifts by a further week.

── Week 1: Unplanned Deload — volume -38%, zero quality cardio, upper-body lift focus, protect sleep above all ──
  Mon
    AM: [run] Easy Z2 Run — 35min aerobic flush — Short, truly easy aerobic effort to maintain movement without adding fatigue load; starting the deload week gently after accumulated fatigue. 
      • WARMUP Walk/jog warmup 5min Z1
      • WORK Easy Zone 2 run — conversational pace 30min @ 5:50/km Z2
      • COOLDOWN Walk cooldown 5min Z1
  Tue
    PM: [strength] Upper Body — horizontal push/pull, RPE 6-7 — Upper-body accessory focus maintains training stimulus while completely unloading fatigued legs; RPE capped at 7 to support recovery without adding systemic stress. 
      • WARMUP Band pull-aparts + arm circles Band pull-aparts 2×15 RPE4
      • WORK Flat dumbbell bench press Flat dumbbell bench press 3×10 RPE6
      • WORK Cable seated row Cable seated row 3×10 RPE6
      • WORK Incline dumbbell press Incline dumbbell press 3×10 RPE6
      • WORK Single-arm dumbbell row Single-arm dumbbell row 3×10 RPE6
      • WORK Lateral raises Lateral raises 3×12 RPE6
      • WORK Face pulls Face pulls 3×15 RPE6
      • COOLDOWN Chest + lat stretch 5min
  Wed
    AM: [bike] Active Recovery Spin — 40min Zone 1-2 — Low-impact cycling promotes blood flow and metabolic clearance in fatigued legs without adding meaningful training stress. 
      • WARMUP Easy spin, no resistance 5min Z1 45% FTP
      • WORK Steady Zone 1-2 spin 30min Z2 58% FTP
      • COOLDOWN Reduce resistance, easy spin 5min Z1 45% FTP
  Thu (REST)
    (full rest)
  Fri
    PM: [strength] Upper Body — vertical push/pull + core, RPE 6-7 — Second weekly upper-body session maintains frequency and training stimulus while legs continue to recover; vertical push/pull complements Tuesday's horizontal emphasis. 
      • WARMUP Shoulder CARs + thoracic rotation 6min
      • WORK Seated dumbbell overhead press Seated dumbbell overhead press 3×10 RPE6
      • WORK Lat pulldown Lat pulldown 3×10 RPE6
      • WORK Cable chest fly Cable chest fly 3×12 RPE6
      • WORK Straight-arm pulldown Straight-arm pulldown 3×12 RPE6
      • WORK Plank holds Plank 3×1 RPE5
      • WORK Dead bug Dead bug 3×10 RPE5
      • COOLDOWN Shoulder + lat stretch 5min
  Sat
    AM: [run] Deload Long Run — 14km easy Z2 — Preserves the long-run habit and aerobic stimulus at a reduced distance appropriate for a deload week; easy pace prevents additional fatigue accumulation. 
      • WARMUP Brisk walk / light jog 7min Z1
      • WORK Easy Zone 2 long run 13.20km @ 5:55/km Z2
      • COOLDOWN Walk + dynamic stretch 7min Z1
  Sun
    AM: [run] Easy Z2 Run — 30min recovery jog — Short aerobic run closes the deload week with minimal fatigue addition; nasal-breathing test acts as a biofeedback check on true Zone 2 effort. 
      • WARMUP Walk 5min Z1
      • WORK Easy Zone 2 run — nasal breathing check 25min @ 5:55/km Z2
      • COOLDOWN Walk + full-body stretch 5min Z1

── Week 2: Cautious Reload — volume held at deload level, one quality run only if HRV ≥52 and sleep ≥6.5h trend; lower-body lift reintroduced at RPE 7 ──
  Mon
    AM: [run] Easy Z2 Run — 40min aerobic base — Slightly longer than the deload Monday to test recovery without overcommitting; still strictly Zone 2, building confidence before the quality session later in the week. 
      • WARMUP Walk/jog warmup 5min Z1
      • WORK Easy Zone 2 run 35min @ 5:45/km Z2
      • COOLDOWN Walk + stretch 5min Z1
  Tue
    PM: [strength] Full Body — lower emphasis reintro, RPE 7 max — Reintroduces lower-body loading at conservative intensity after a week of upper-only work; placed Tuesday to give 48h+ clearance before any quality run later in the week. 
      • WARMUP Hip circles + leg swings + goblet squat warm-up 7min
      • WORK Goblet squat Goblet squat 3×10 @ 20kg RPE6
      • WORK Romanian deadlift — moderate load Romanian deadlift 3×10 @ 50kg RPE7
      • WORK Flat dumbbell bench press Flat dumbbell bench press 3×10 RPE7
      • WORK Cable seated row Cable seated row 3×10 RPE7
      • WORK Bulgarian split squat — bodyweight only Bulgarian split squat 2×8 RPE6
      • COOLDOWN Hip flexor + hamstring stretch 6min
  Wed
    AM: [bike] Active Recovery Spin — 45min Zone 1-2 — Low-intensity cycling on the day after reintroducing lower-body lifting aids recovery and keeps aerobic base ticking over without stressing fatigued musculature. 
      • WARMUP Easy spin 5min Z1 45% FTP
      • WORK Steady Zone 2 spin 35min Z2 60% FTP
      • COOLDOWN Wind down spin 5min Z1 45% FTP
  Thu (REST)
    (full rest)
  Fri
    AM: [run] Threshold Run — 3×5min @ ~4:15/km, 2min jog (conditional) — Single quality session of the week, placed Friday after a full rest day; 48h+ clear from Tuesday's lift. Short reps with generous recovery ease back into intensity without overloading still-recovering systems. 
      • WARMUP Easy jog warmup 15min @ 5:55/km Z2
      • 3× (  WORK Threshold rep @ ~4:15/km 5min @ 4:15/km Z4 +   RECOVERY Easy jog recovery 2min @ 6:00/km Z2)
      • COOLDOWN Easy jog + walk cooldown 10min @ 6:10/km Z1
  Sat
    AM: [run] Long Run — 14km easy Z2, steady aerobic — Maintains long-run aerobic stimulus at the same distance as the deload week, respecting the 0% weekly volume increase constraint while the athlete fully stabilizes. 
      • WARMUP Walk + easy jog 7min Z1
      • WORK Easy Zone 2 long run 13.20km @ 5:50/km Z2
      • COOLDOWN Walk + full-body stretch 7min Z1
  Sun
    PM: [strength] Upper Body + Light Core — vertical focus, RPE 7 — Second weekly strength session closes the week on a low-fatigue modality after Saturday's long run; upper focus avoids adding leg stress before the next training cycle begins. 
      • WARMUP Band pull-aparts + thoracic extension 6min
      • WORK Seated dumbbell overhead press Seated dumbbell overhead press 3×10 RPE7
      • WORK Lat pulldown Lat pulldown 3×10 RPE7
      • WORK Incline dumbbell press Incline dumbbell press 3×10 RPE7
      • WORK Single-arm dumbbell row Single-arm dumbbell row 3×10 RPE7
      • WORK Pallof press Pallof press 3×10 RPE5
      • COOLDOWN Chest, lat, and hip flexor stretch 6min
